plan.goal: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.
plan.title: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.

acceptance.label: Relevant daemon, bridge, launcher, runtime-state, and auth/authority code owners are identified with specific failure hypotheses.
The plan names where to instrument exit, restart, authority lookup, and bridge-auth invalidation paths.

node.summary: Map the code paths for daemon spawn, restart, shutdown, bridge reconnect, runtime-state persistence, authority lookup, credential registry, and bridge-auth binding so the unexplained death and stale-auth behavior have concrete owners and hypotheses.
node.title: Trace daemon lifecycle, supervisor, and authority persistence paths in code

plan.goal: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.
plan.title: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.

acceptance.label: Future daemon exits leave durable structured logs or persisted state indicating exit path and restart context.
Launcher or supervisor paths capture enough metadata to distinguish crash, clean shutdown, and external kill scenarios.

node.summary: Instrument daemon startup, normal shutdown, panic/fatal exit paths, supervisor/launcher restart accounting, and runtime-state persistence so future daemon disappearance leaves durable structured evidence with timestamps, exit reason, last known health, and relevant restart metadata.
node.title: Add durable daemon crash and shutdown observability

plan.goal: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.
plan.title: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.

acceptance.label: The bridge can detect dead upstream daemon state and trigger a safe restart or respawn flow instead of only retrying a stale URI.
Recovery behavior is bounded and avoids silent infinite churn or duplicate-daemon storms.

node.summary: Teach the bridge to escalate from reconnect retries to explicit liveness checks, stale-runtime-state detection, safe daemon restart or respawn, and bounded retry/backoff so an upstream daemon death converges back to a healthy connection without manual intervention.
node.title: Make the bridge self-heal dead upstream daemons instead of retrying a stale URI indefinitely

plan.goal: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.
plan.title: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.

acceptance.label: Bridge auth state is revalidated against the live authority after restart or on first authoritative mutation.
Stale credential bindings are cleared or refreshed automatically, or fail with accurate user-facing state instead of misleading readiness.

node.summary: Harden bridge auth so restart or authority changes cannot leave prism://bridge/auth claiming readiness while authoritative mutations fail. Add credential revalidation on adopt and on mutation_auth_failed, plus clear next actions for refresh or re-login.
node.title: Revalidate or invalidate stale bridge auth bindings after daemon or authority changes

plan.goal: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.
plan.title: Trace the daemon death and auth-drift failure paths in code, add durable observability so future daemon exits are diagnosable, and harden the bridge so dead daemons and stale credentials recover automatically or fail with actionable state.

acceptance.label: Injected daemon death leaves durable forensic evidence and the bridge converges back to a healthy daemon connection.
Injected stale-auth scenarios produce correct bridge state and successful recovery or accurate user-facing remediation.

node.summary: Add validation scenarios that kill or interrupt the daemon, simulate stale runtime-state/URI conditions, and exercise stale or missing credentials so the new observability and recovery flows are proven under realistic failure injection.
node.title: Validate crash logging, bridge auto-recovery, and stale-auth recovery with injected failures

node.summary: Traced the daemon lifecycle, bridge reconnect, auth injection, and principal-registry paths; confirmed missing exit instrumentation, stale runtime-state pruning without observability, bridge retrying dead upstream URIs without restart escalation, and bridge auth reporting bound state without live authority validation.
node.title: Trace daemon lifecycle, supervisor, and authority persistence paths in code

acceptance.label: Future daemon exits leave durable structured logs or persisted state indicating exit path and restart context.
Launcher or supervisor paths capture enough metadata to distinguish crash, clean shutdown, and external kill scenarios.

node.summary: Implement durable daemon lifecycle observability in runtime state and top-level logging, and record dead-daemon detections during bridge reconnects so future daemon disappearances leave actionable evidence.
node.title: Add durable daemon crash and shutdown observability

acceptance.label: Future daemon exits leave durable structured logs or persisted state indicating exit path and restart context.
Launcher or supervisor paths capture enough metadata to distinguish crash, clean shutdown, and external kill scenarios.

node.summary: Added durable daemon lifecycle observability in runtime state and top-level logging, recorded bridge upstream connection failures during warmup and reconnect, rebuilt the release binaries, and restarted the live daemon successfully. Targeted prism-mcp tests passed; full workspace cargo test is still blocked by unrelated prism-store pragma-count assertions.
node.title: Add durable daemon crash and shutdown observability

acceptance.label: The bridge can detect dead upstream daemon state and trigger a safe restart or respawn flow instead of only retrying a stale URI.
Recovery behavior is bounded and avoids silent infinite churn or duplicate-daemon storms.

node.summary: Implement bridge-side self-healing by escalating dead upstream reconnects into daemon resolution or restart instead of retrying a stale URI indefinitely, while preserving clear diagnostics when recovery still fails.
node.title: Make the bridge self-heal dead upstream daemons instead of retrying a stale URI indefinitely

acceptance.label: The bridge can detect dead upstream daemon state and trigger a safe restart or respawn flow instead of only retrying a stale URI.
Recovery behavior is bounded and avoids silent infinite churn or duplicate-daemon storms.

node.summary: Bootstrapped bridges now resolve reconnect targets through daemon resolution instead of retrying stale URI-file values indefinitely, targeted reconnect tests passed, and the updated release daemon restarted healthy. A full workspace cargo test rerun hit the known flaky runtime-sync timing test again, but the isolated rerun passed and validation was treated as successful under repo policy.
node.title: Make the bridge self-heal dead upstream daemons instead of retrying a stale URI indefinitely

acceptance.label: Bridge auth state is revalidated against the live authority after restart or on first authoritative mutation.

acceptance.label: Bridge auth state is revalidated against the live authority after restart or on first authoritative mutation.
